frameworks.name: high-level abstract scene coding
frameworks.one_line: uniformity is represented as a whole-scene property in prefrontal cortex

frameworks.name: perceptual filling-in
frameworks.one_line: early-visual completion of missing peripheral detail, argued against here

frameworks.name: feedforward convolutional neural network
frameworks.one_line: intermediate/late CNN layers as a model of uniformity representation

core_question: Where in the brain is the illusory percept of peripheral visual uniformity represented?
key_assumptions: subjective uniformity in peripheral vision is partly illusory given coarse peripheral processing
illusory percepts can be decoded from voxel patterns
calibrated Gabor displays can dissociate uniform vs incoherent percepts
manipulations: peripheral Gabor patches: identical vs heterogeneous orientations
stimulus context: psychophysical Gabor display vs naturalistic movies
comparison of voxel patterns to layers of a feedforward CNN
key_findings: lateral prefrontal cortex voxel patterns predicted perceived uniformity
these patterns generalized to scene uniformity in naturalistic movies
intermediate and late layers of a feedforward CNN showed similar uniformity representations
interpretations: uniformity perception involves high-level coding of abstract whole-scene properties
this mechanism is distinct from early-visual filling-in of specific details
